fields.description: Positions are 1-indexed and counted on the full barcode. Example: 3310100010009 => prefix 331, item code 3310100, weight 1.000, check digit 9.
fields.label: Barcode Structure

fields.description: Enable when your barcode starts with a fixed prefix value (for example, 331).
fields.label: Prefix included in barcode

fields.description: Prefix length (for example, prefix 331 => length 3). Item/weight start positions still use full barcode positions.
fields.label: Prefix length

fields.description: Fixed starting value of all scale barcodes (for example, 331). If set, barcodes not starting with this value are ignored.
fields.label: Prefix

fields.description: 1-indexed start position of item code in the full barcode. Example: for item code 3310100 in 3310100010009, start is 1.
fields.label: Item code starting position

fields.description: Total item code digits to extract. Example: item code 3310100 => digits 7.
fields.label: Item code digits

fields.description: Weight value is parsed as: [weight digits].[weight decimals].
fields.label: Weight

fields.description: 1-indexed start position of weight segment. Example: in 3310100010009, weight starts at position 8.
fields.label: Weight starting position

fields.description: Digits before decimal. Example: for 1.000, use 1 (or 2 if your scale encodes 01.000).
fields.label: Weight digits

fields.description: Enable only if barcode also contains price segment.
fields.label: Price

fields.description: When enabled, price is extracted using the price start/digits/decimals fields below.
fields.label: Price included in barcode

fields.description: Starting position (1-indexed) of the price segment.
fields.label: Price starting position

fields.description: Number of digits representing the price before decimal places.
fields.label: Price digits

fields.description: Number of decimal places encoded for the price.
fields.label: Price decimals
